content:
- Finished Task 01 completely and pushed commit e28b0e5.
- Final required gates were green: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint. The first ultra-long run had one failing HA integrity scenario, but the isolated rerun and then the full make test-long rerun both passed, so the committed state includes a green full-profile run.
- Task markdown now has status completed and passes=true, Ralph task_switch.sh has been run, and the repo is clean after push.

- Read current progress log and active/done task summaries.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks in .ralph/current_tasks.md; all bug entries are in current_tasks_done.md with passes=true.
- Inspected all active task files for header priority and blocked_by tags; none of the selectable non-meta tasks declare blockers, and only the code-hygiene task is explicitly low priority.
- The strongest next-step candidate is the tracing-based-logging story task 02, because story task 01 is already done and tasks 03/04 are downstream follow-ups in the same migration chain.
- Next action: write .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/02-task-migrate-runtime-process-and-api-to-owned-typed-events.md into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Loaded prior progress and confirmed the active task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Active task is story-tracing-based-logging task 02 about migrating runtime/process/API logging to owned typed tracing events.
- Next I am reading the task file itself and then the relevant runtime/process/API modules so I can write a full plan into the task markdown and stop at the required TO BE VERIFIED stage.

- Started current task by reading the progress log and confirmed the active task context is the typed tracing event migration.
- Scanned src/process/worker.rs and src/api/worker.rs for emit sites; both files still use many log.emit_event/raw record calls around worker lifecycle, process execution, API auth, API responses, and TLS handling.
- Next I am reading the concrete functions and tests to summarize each site, its current event name/attributes, likely ownership bucket, and which assertions/helpers are tied to current event payloads.

- Read prior progress and confirmed the active investigation is the tracing/logging migration story focused on owned typed events for runtime/process/API.
- Started locating the shared event infrastructure in src/logging and searching for concrete enum/builder/decoder usage sites in src and tests.
- Next I need to read the central event definitions plus any ingestion/tailer/test helper code so I can summarize exactly what already exists without drifting into unrelated modules.

- Read src/logging/event.rs and src/logging/mod.rs. The central shared typed-event surface currently appears to be AppEvent/AppEventHeader/StructuredFields plus a cfg(test) decoder.
- Search results so far do not show runtime/process/api-specific event enums or decoder types by name, so I am verifying actual call sites before stating that absence.
- Next I am enumerating every AppEvent/AppEventHeader usage and checking surrounding tests/helpers in logging/tailer/postgres ingest.

- Inspected src/runtime/node.rs for current emission sites and traced each startup-related log path.
- Confirmed node.rs currently uses three styles: typed app events via log.emit_event, an untyped app log via emit_startup_phase/log.emit, and raw subprocess forwarding via emit_startup_subprocess_line/log.emit_raw_record.
- Found direct node.rs test coverage only for plan_startup_with_probe event names (data_dir.inspected, dcs_cache_probe, mode_selected); execute_startup/run_startup_job emissions are not directly tested here. Supporting logging tests cover generic AppEvent encoding and SubprocessLineRecord encoding.
- Next if context continues on this task: compare these node.rs ownership patterns against process worker and any target typed-event migration plan.

- Mapped runtime/process/api call sites: they currently call LogHandle::emit_event/emit_raw_record with inline EventMeta::new values rather than reusing domain-specific shared event enums/builders.
- Read logging/raw_record.rs and logging/postgres_ingest.rs; shared builders also exist for subprocess/raw/postgres line normalization, which is adjacent but distinct from the app typed-event path.
- Next I am collecting exact line references for the final inventory, including test-only helpers like decode_app_event and TestSink.

- Finished inspection of the typed event infrastructure introduced for the tracing/logging migration.
- Confirmed the shared reusable surface lives in src/logging/{mod,event,raw_record}. Runtime/process/api currently use generic LogHandle::emit_event plus inline EventMeta::new string literals; there are no shared runtime/process/api-specific event enums/builders/decoders yet.
- Final response will inventory the existing shared enums/builders/decoders and the test helpers (TestSink, decode_app_event, plus local test_log_handle wrappers) with exact file references.

- Read the concrete emit sites in src/process/worker.rs and src/api/worker.rs plus the task text for ownership vocabulary.
- Clear split: process `run`/`step_once` and API `run` plus parts of `step_once` are orchestration boundaries; `start_job`, `tick_active_job`, `emit_api_auth_decision`, and `accept_connection` own most execution-result or transport-failure events; `emit_subprocess_line` is already on the raw-record path via SubprocessLineRecord.
- Test coupling is narrow in these files: process test `step_once_emits_request_received_and_job_started` and API test `auth_decision_logs_do_not_leak_bearer_token` both still inspect raw log records and `event.name`; local helpers `test_log_handle`, `test_ctx_with_log`, `build_ctx_with_config_and_log`, and TestSink snapshot/matcher access are the main migration points.

- Read the active tracing/logging task and the Task 01 completed plan to match the expected markdown structure.
- Inspected src/runtime/node.rs, src/process/worker.rs, src/api/worker.rs, current logging primitives, and the local tests that still assert on record.attributes["event.name"].
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/02-task-migrate-runtime-process-and-api-to-owned-typed-events.md and ended it with TO BE VERIFIED as instructed.
- Important open review point for the next pass: runtime startup ownership is the main place to pressure-test, especially whether data-dir/DCS probe events belong in small operation wrappers or should remain planner-owned; a late explorer summary argued for keeping more of them as orchestration-boundary events.
- Stopping now without executing implementation, verification, or broader edits because the task instructions explicitly require quitting immediately at the TO BE VERIFIED stage.

- Late explorer summary arrived after I had already stopped at TO BE VERIFIED.
- Useful finding: the shared typed event surface already exists only in src/logging/{mod,event,raw_record}.rs via AppEvent/AppEventHeader/StructuredFields/decode_app_event/SubprocessLineRecord.
- There are no domain-specific runtime/process/api typed event enums or builders yet; those will need to be introduced during execution rather than assumed to already exist.
- This late result supports the current plan shape but is a good review constraint for the next pass: prefer thin domain-specific typed builders on top of the existing logging contract rather than inventing a second shared layer.

- Read startup progress log and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/02-task-migrate-runtime-process-and-api-to-owned-typed-events.md.
- About to inspect the task file content to decide whether I need to create the initial plan or perform the skeptical TO BE VERIFIED review pass.

- Read the active task markdown and found it already at TO BE VERIFIED, so I performed the required skeptical review pass instead of executing code.
- Re-read the current runtime/process/api logging code to pressure-test ownership boundaries against the real control flow.
- Changed the plan in .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/02-task-migrate-runtime-process-and-api-to-owned-typed-events.md in two substantive ways: keep runtime startup action result events in execute_startup because it owns sequence/index semantics, and keep process preflight noop/failure events in start_job because it owns the state transition even if helper functions compute the underlying fact.
- Replaced the end marker with NOW EXECUTE. Next engineer/turn should execute the plan directly without another exploration pass.

- Read startup progress log and current task pointer.
- Confirmed active task is .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/02-task-migrate-runtime-process-and-api-to-owned-typed-events.md.
- Next I am reading the task markdown to determine whether this turn should review the plan or execute it.

- Executed the runtime/process/api migration plan rather than re-planning because the task file already said NOW EXECUTE.
- Replaced ad hoc app-event map assembly in src/runtime/node.rs, src/process/worker.rs, and src/api/worker.rs with domain-specific typed AppEvent helpers and emit_app_event calls.
- Started updating affected tests to use decode_app_event instead of raw event.name attribute lookups; next step is compile/test and fix any fallout.

- Finished executing the typed logging migration for runtime/process/api and updated the active task markdown to completed with passes=true.
- Verified the final tree with make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint; all passed on the final state.
- Next immediate step is the Ralph handoff sequence: run task_switch.sh, commit all tracked changes including .ralph evidence/progress/task files, and push.
